business.headline: Groceries, fuel, school fees: Is your monthly budget about to get tighter?School fees, petrol, vegetables, utility bills—the list of monthly expenses never seems to end. As inflation rises, many families are asking the same question: is their budget about to get even tighter?

Inflation rises to 3.93% for May as West Asia conflict pushes up fuel and food pricesFood inflation continued to move higher in May. It stood at 4.78%, up from 4.20% in April, showing that prices of food items are rising faster than they were a month earlier.

Markets rally on easing West Asia tensions, oil falling below $88The S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Salary mismatch to missed interest: 7 ITR mistakes that can trigger tax noticesFiling your ITR may seem straightforward, but even a minor reporting error can attract the attention of the tax department. Here are seven mistakes you should watch out for.

The world's biggest IPO starts trading today. Is Elon Musk's SpaceX overvalued?SpaceX begins trading on Nasdaq after a record $75 billion IPO and a $1.75 trillion valuation. The debut has sharpened questions over valuation, Musk's control, xAI losses and broader market liquidity.

FD rates up to 8.10%: Check these small finance banks offering the highest returnsIn a world of rising uncertainty, guaranteed returns are becoming more valuable. Here's a look at the small finance banks offering FD rates as high as 8.10% this month.

Govt's new petrol, diesel order: Who is affected, and why was it introduced?The Centre has restricted industrial and commercial bulk fuel purchases from retail pumps. The move aims to protect pump supplies for ordinary consumers amid a wide retail-bulk price gap.

Earning Rs 20 lakh? Claim these tax benefits under the new tax regimeThink the new tax regime leaves no room for tax savings? If you earn Rs 20 lakh a year, you may be surprised to learn that several tax-friendly benefits are still available.
Gold demand picks up despite PM's appeal to avoid buyingAt the time of writing at 11.45 am, MCX gold was trading at Rs 1,49,942, up Rs 1,021, while silver stood at Rs 2,42,232, higher by Rs 2,579.

India may let fiscal deficit widen as US-Iran war raises oil costsIndia may allow its fiscal deficit to widen to 4.8% of GDP as the Iran war raises oil-linked costs. The move reflects pressure from higher subsidies, costlier imports and possible spending cuts.

Inflation fears return: Will Indians have to spend more on everyday items?Rising global oil prices and stronger US inflation are reviving concerns over India's household costs. Economists and the RBI say risks are building even without an immediate inflation shock.

Sensex jumped 1,700 points: 3 reasons why stock market rose todayThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Markets surge as crude oil falls below $90 amid hopes of US-Iran breakthroughThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 873.88 points, or 1.18%, to 74,706.43 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 climbed 236.40 points, or 1.02%, to 23,398.00.

World's first trillionaire Elon Musk is now richer than Taiwan after SpaceX IPOElon Musk became the world's first trillionaire after SpaceX's record IPO lifted his holdings. The milestone pushed his fortune past Taiwan's economy and widened the gap with every other billionaire.

Centre restricts bulk industrial fuel purchases at petrol pumpsThe government has barred industrial, commercial and institutional users from buying petrol and diesel at retail pumps. The move seeks to prevent diversion, curb shortages and protect supplies for ordinary vehicle owners.

US drug agency puts Dabur products on import alert after inspection flags lapsesThe US FDA has placed drugs manufactured at Dabur India's Dadra and Nagar Haveli factory under an import alert after an inspection found data integrity and maintenance lapses.

Steady adjustment of rupee is okay, a free fall is not: Raghuram RajanIn an exclusive interview with India Today TV, former Reserve Bank of India Governor Raghuram Rajan shares his thoughts on how to deal with the ongoing conflict in West Asia's impact on the global and Indian economies and more.

Something is off: Raghuram Rajan questions India's growth narrative"Something is off" with India's economy, former RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan said, questioning whether official GDP figures showing growth of more than 7 per cent accurately reflect conditions on the ground.

India to stay world's fastest-growing major economy, growth at 6.6%: World BankIndia will remain the world's fastest-growing major economy, with growth expected to slow from 7.7% in FY2025-26 to 6.6% in FY2026-27 before recovering to 7.2% the following year.

Why alcohol sales are set to slow globally over next 10 years but grow in IndiaAs global alcohol consumption enters a phase of slowdown, India appears to be charting a very different growth path. What lies behind this contrasting trend?

entertainment.headline: Taylor Swift inducted into Songwriters Hall of Fame, thanks family in tearful speech Taylor Swift thanked her family and Steven Spielberg as she accepted her Songwriters Hall of Fame honour in New York. The speech framed her record-setting induction at 36 as a reflection on sacrifice, storytelling and endurance.

Box office: Governor vs Main Vaapas vs Bharat Bhhagya and why Haunted 3D is surprise The industry witnessed an unusually crowded box-office clash on Friday as nine films hit theatres simultaneously. With Governor, Main Vaapas Aaunga and Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata leading the race, trade experts believe the weekend winner will ultimately be decided by word-of-mouth rather than star power.

Gram Chikitsalay 2 trailer: Amol Parashar's health mission faces new hurdles The trailer of Gram Chikitsalay 2 sends Dr Prabhat, played by Amol Parashar, back to Bhathkandi, where reviving the Primary Health Centre remains an uphill task. It sets up a season shaped by village politics, distrust and shortages that keep testing rural healthcare.

Did you know 94-year-old Singeetham Srinivasa Rao wrapped Sing Geetham in 77 days? Director Singeetham Srinivasa Rao completed Sing Geetham in 77 shooting days at the age of 94. The film's remote direction, health setback and all-sung narrative underline its unusual production.

Sing Geetham review: A grandfather's tale told by 94-year-old master storytellerRelease Date:Fri Jun 12, 2026 Sing Geetham review: Singeetham Srinivasa Rao's Sing Geetham follows a village where people are cursed to sing every word after greed destroys its last sacred tree. The musical fantasy turns that eccentric premise into a sincere fable about nature, greed and human change.

Misogyny, abuse, clicks: How the Pranit More row exposes stand-up comedy's crisis The controversy around comedian Pranit More's viral crowd-work clip should spark a larger debate about the state of stand-up comedy. As misogyny, profanity and shock value increasingly drive engagement, are jokes being replaced by content designed purely for clicks?

Katrina Kaif eyes comeback with Chandni Bar 2, joins Tabu in sequel: Report Katrina Kaif is reportedly in talks for Madhur Bhandarkar's Chandni Bar 2. The sequel is said to revisit the red light area and crime ecosystem, according to sources.

Singer Chinmayi calls out TVK and DMK over platforming sexual abuser Vedan Singer Chinmayi Sripaada criticised Vijay's party TVK, MLA Rajmohan and DMK for platforming rapper Vedan despite past sexual allegations against him. She said women who speak up against influential men are often left without support while such figures continue to be platformed.

Shahid Kapoor reacts as Anupama Chopra alleges paid negativity against Alia Bhatt Shahid Kapoor has responded after Anupama Chopra's remarks about an alleged paid campaign targeting Alia Bhatt sparked widespread discussion. Here's what the actor said and why the conversation is making headlines.

After confirming political entry, actor Raghava Lawrence asks fans to say yes or no Actor-director Raghava Lawrence shared an 18-minute video on X to explain his political interest and seek a yes-or-no response from fans. Earlier, in an X post, he said that several circumstances had led him to consider entering politics.

Kangana flags Bollywood's 'Pakistan prem', links Ranveer's Don 3 row to Dhurandhar Asked about Ranveer Singh being targeted, Kangana Ranaut linked the chatter to Dhurandhar. She said the reaction reflected discomfort over films that challenge older narratives on Pakistan.

Salman Khan approaches High Court against Kala Hiran, alleges reputation damage Salman Khan has approached the Delhi High Court against Kala Hiran: Battle for Legacy film over alleged use of his persona. He says the film and its promotions could affect ongoing proceedings and damage his reputation.

Heer Sara review: Well-intentioned slice-of-life film loses steam in familiar tropesRelease Date:Fri Jun 12, 2026 Heer Sara movie review: In trying to balance progressive ideals with slice-of-life realism, Heer Sara foregrounds sisterhood, queer acceptance and emotional estrangement. But can a well-intended story alone mask pacing issues and familiar tropes?

Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata X reviews: Fans say every actor deserves an award Kangana Ranaut's Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata opened in theatres on 12 June to largely positive reactions on X. The film has drawn attention for dramatising the courage of Cama Hospital nurses during the 26/11 attacks.

Heart Of The Beast trailer sees Brad Pitt stranded in Alaska after devastating crash Actor Brad Pitt's Heart of the Beast trailer follows a crash survivor and his loyal service dog through the Alaskan wilderness, setting up a survival story ahead of the film's September release.

Kangana Ranaut says women-led cinema is the result of generations of contributions Kangana Ranaut reflects on the rise of women-led films in Bollywood, crediting generations of women artists and pioneers for driving change in the industry ahead of the release of Bharat Bhagya Vidhata.

Court pulls up Salman Khan's neighbour over social media posts in property dispute The Bombay High Court heard Salman Khan's plea against his Panvel farmhouse neighbour over social media posts. The Bench questioned why grievances were not pursued with authorities and suggested the posts be deleted.

Disclosure Day review: Spielberg's latest is messy, thoughtful and timelyRelease Date:Fri Jun 12, 2026 Steven Spielberg's Disclosure Day follows a whistleblower and a meteorologist drawn into a race over hidden evidence of alien contact. The film turns that chase into a question about secrecy, truth and whether humanity can bear disclosure.

Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata review: Kangana is flame in film that could've been a fireRelease Date:Fri Jun 12, 2026 Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata review: Inspired by the 26/11 Cama Hospital attack, Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata tells the story of unsung heroes who kept Mumbai going during one of its darkest nights. Led by a powerful Kangana Ranaut, the film shines a light on nurses, security guards and ordinary citizens, even as its one-woman focus leaves many stories untold.

general news.headline: European weapons used to attack India: Jaishankar silences West on Russian oilExternal Affairs Minister Subrahmanyam Jaishankar strongly defended India's Russian oil purchases at the Kultaranta Talks in Finland. He said the West had no moral authority to lecture India as weapons from European countries had been used against it while Delhi never posed any threat to Europe.

IMD confirms El Nino, warns of intensifying conditions through monsoonFor India, it has historically been linked with weaker monsoon rainfall, higher temperatures, prolonged dry spells and an increased risk of drought in some years.

Living with scars, unanswered questions: Lone Air India crash survivor a year onA year after the Air India AI171 crash that claimed 260 lives, lone survivor Viswashkumar Ramesh is still struggling with trauma, the loss of his brother, unanswered questions about the disaster, and the financial hardships that followed.

Entertainment shouldn't cross dignity limits: Fadnavis on Rs 370 biryani rowDevendra Fadnavis slammed the 'Rs 370 biryani' remark, saying that while the Constitution guarantees freedom of expression, entertainment should not come at the cost of human dignity or cross the boundaries of decency.

The Fab Four had giants. Kane Williamson is the one they all lovedHe averaged nearly 50 across formats, won a World Test Championship, and came agonisingly close to a World Cup. But Kane Williamson's greatest contribution to cricket was something no scorecard ever captured.

Supreme Court dismisses Meenakshi Natarajan's plea against RS nomination rejectionThe Supreme Court's division bench held that established legal principles prohibit judicial interference in the electoral process while it is underway, and rejected Natarajan's plea challenging the nomination process over the alleged non-disclosure of a criminal case in Telangana.

India summons US diplomat for second time over attacks on ships with Indian crewThe American diplomat was called in by the Additional Secretary (Americas) in the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA), as New Delhi stepped up its diplomatic response to the escalating threat to commercial shipping in the Gulf region.

Indian shooting legend and coach Jaspal Rana dies at 49Renowned Indian shooting coach and former Asian Games gold medallist Jaspal Rana died at the age of 49 after suffering cardiac complications that first surfaced during his return journey from Munich to Delhi following the ISSF World Cup.

Med student jokes about cadaver penis, Germans bury skeleton of Indian with honoursGerman school students buried a skeleton from their biology lab after discovering it was likely of an Indian man exported during the colonial-era bone trade. This came even as a joke on cadavers in medical colleges by an Indian medical student, Sejal Pawar, triggered outrage.

Kane Williamson retires: New Zealand great walks away on his own termsFormer New Zealand captain Kane Williamson, one of the members of the Fab Four of modern cricket, has announced his retirement. The 35-year-old has hung up his boots midway through a Test series in England.

NEET UG 2026 candidates to get 15 extra minutes, NTA introduces changesNTA has announced candidate-friendly changes for NEET UG 2026, including a 195-minute exam window and a revised question paper layout. The move adds more rough-work space and is aimed at making the exam smoother for candidates.

How rescuers broke through hostel walls, battled fire to find AI171 crash survivorsA year after the AI171 crash, Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation chief Banchanidhi Pani recounted how rescuers broke through hostel walls and firefighters battled intense heat to reach the crashed plane to search for survivors.

Indira Gandhi was dumped by Congress twice. Then...As reports suggest, former West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee could lose control of the Trinamool Congress she founded. There's an intriguing parallel. Former PM Indira Gandhi was expelled from her party twice. Once while she was in power. This is what she did and how she emerged a winner both times.

Amazon says its data centres used over 9 billion litres of water in 2025 aloneAmazon has revealed that its data centres consumed 2.5 billion gallons (over 9 billion litres) of water last year alone. However, the company claims that its data centres are actually more efficient than those of the competition, including Microsoft, Google, and Meta.

FIFA under fire as empty seats overshadow South Korea's comeback winWorld Cup: FIFA reported an attendance of 44,985 for the Group A match between South Korea and the Czech Republic in Guadalajara. Still, large sections of empty seats at Estadio Akron reignited concerns over ticket prices and fan demand at the expanded 48-team tournament.

Cadaver joke row triggers wider ethics debate in medical collegesA KEM Hospital student's joke about a male cadaver at a comedy show triggered outrage and police action. Doctors say the episode threatens trust in body donation and underscores the need for dignity online and in anatomy labs.

This Padma Shri scientist proved India carries world's oldest DNA outside AfricaPadma Shri awardee Dr Kumarasamy Thangaraj helped prove that Andaman tribes carry one of the world's oldest human genetic lineages outside Africa. His work changed how scientists understand Indian DNA, medicine, migration and ancestry. Two decades later, GenomeIndia is revealing just how much of India's genetic story the world had overlooked.

Live: Nuke talks within 60 days after peace deal signing, says Iran's state mediaUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The apparent diplomatic breakthrough announced by US President Donald Trump was quickly clouded by uncertainty after Iranian media pushed back against his claims that key points of an agreement had already been approved. Trump said he cancelled planned strikes on Iran because discussions had reached the highest levels of the Iranian leadership and had secured broad regional backing. However, Iran's semi-official Fars News Agency later cited a source close to the negotiating team as saying that Tehran had not approved any text for a preliminary memorandum of understanding with Washington. The conflicting accounts left the true state of negotiations unclear, even as both sides continued indirect contacts. Trump earlier vowed to take Kharg Island, which handles roughly 90% of Iran's crude exports. Plans to capture the island have been drawn up for months but continuously shelved because it was considered too risky. Iranian officials have maintained that the country is prepared for any escalation and have rejected suggestions that military pressure would force concessions. Meanwhile, reports from Iranian and Western officials indicate that negotiations remain active, with major issues such as sanctions relief, frozen Iranian assets and regional security arrangements still unresolved. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates.

Bibi kept in dark: Netanyahu was unaware of Trump's Iran deal announcementDonald Trump announced a potential agreement with Iran and called off planned military strikes against Tehran, but Benjamin Netanyahu was unaware of it.

Is director Chinmay Mandlekar's Governor: The Silent Saviour worth catching for the heart beneath the headlines? Here is our review.

Mumbai rain: Monsoon on a march then why is it not raining in Maharashtra?As much of India watches the progress of the southwest monsoon, one of the country's most rain-dependent cities is still waiting for the season to fully arrive. What's delaying the monsoon?

The film turns that chase into a question about secrecy, truth and whether humanity can bear disclosure.

sports.headline: The Fab Four had giants. Kane Williamson is the one they all loved He averaged nearly 50 across formats, won a World Test Championship, and came agonisingly close to a World Cup. But Kane Williamson's greatest contribution to cricket was something no scorecard ever captured.

India vs Afghanistan 1st ODI: When and where to watch, squads and key updates India move from a one-sided Test win into a three-match ODI series against Afghanistan. The series opens a new season for Shubman Gill's side and gives Afghanistan a first bilateral ODI shot at India.

Kane Williamson retires: New Zealand great walks away on his own terms Former New Zealand captain Kane Williamson, one of the members of the Fab Four of modern cricket, has announced his retirement. The 35-year-old has hung up his boots midway through a Test series in England.

Don't ignore Rishabh Pant: Ex-pacer warns selectors ahead of 2027 ODI World Cup Irfan Pathan has urged selectors not to write off Rishabh Pant after his white-ball omission. He said Pant can still force a comeback before the 2027 ODI World Cup.

Fever to fervour: Oh overcomes illness to inspire South Korea's opening-day win FIFA World Cup 2026: Oh Hyeon-gyu came off the bench to score late as South Korea beat Czechia 2-1 in their opener. The striker said a 38-degree fever nearly ruled him out, while ciacg Hong Myung-bo praised the side's refusal to give up.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has the potential to be bigger than Virat, Sachin: Dale Steyn Former South Africa pacer Dale Steyn believes teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has the potential to surpass even Sachin Tendulkar and Virat Kohli, while warning that India must manage the youngster carefully to help him fulfil his immense promise.

You'll never get me out: Sachin Tendulkar dares daughter Sara on private jet Sachin Tendulkar turned a private jet cabin into a playful cricket pitch with his family. The clip travelled fast online, underlining how completely the game still shapes his public image.

FIFA under fire as empty seats overshadow South Korea's comeback win World Cup: FIFA reported an attendance of 44,985 for the Group A match between South Korea and the Czech Republic in Guadalajara. Still, large sections of empty seats at Estadio Akron reignited concerns over ticket prices and fan demand at the expanded 48-team tournament.

Opening ceremony: Who is performing, when and where to watch The ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026 opens at Edgbaston with a ceremony featuring the full cast of Wicked before England face Sri Lanka. The staging underlines the tournament's scale, with strong ticket sales and a wider push to present women's cricket as a major global event.

WI vs SL, 1st T20I: Holder, Joseph power West Indies to series-opening win Jason Holder led the charge with the ball before Shai Hope anchored the chase with an unbeaten 65 as West Indies defeated Sri Lanka by seven wickets in the first T20I at Sabina Park on Thursday to take an early lead in the three-match series.

Real Madrid goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois set to retire after FIFA World Cup 2026 FIFA World Cup 2026: Thibaut Courtois has indicated the ongoing World Cup is more likely to be his last tournament with Belgium than not. Any exit would hasten Belgium's transition as younger goalkeepers wait behind a century-capped senior figure.

Brought immense glory to nation: PM Modi condoles shooting legend Jaspal RanaPrime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday expressed grief over the demise of former Asian Games gold medallist Jaspal Rana, describing it as a major loss for Indian sport and paying tribute to his outstanding contributions as both a shooter and coach.

Gwalior's Yashbardhan Chauhan to lead India U-19 on Sri Lanka tourGwalior's Yashvardhan Singh Chauhan will lead India Under-19 on the Sri Lanka tour. His appointment, along with Manal Chauhan's selection, has lifted Chambal cricket and underlined the region's growing pipeline.

Australian Open: PV Sindhu dismantles Chen Su Yu to book semifinal berthThird seed PV Sindhu produced a dominant display to enter the women's singles semi-finals of the Australian Open Super 500, defeating Chinese Taipei's Chen Su Yu 21-6, 21-9 on Friday.

Raul Jimenez lives his World Cup moment after defying the impossibleFIFA World Cup 2026: Six years after a skull fracture threatened his life and career, Raul Jimenez scored his first World Cup goal in Mexico's home opener. It was a moment that transcended football, completing one of the game's most remarkable comeback stories.

Indian shooting legend and coach Jaspal Rana dies at 49Renowned Indian shooting coach and former Asian Games gold medallist Jaspal Rana died at the age of 49 after suffering cardiac complications that first surfaced during his return journey from Munich to Delhi following the ISSF World Cup.

World Cup: Hwang In-beom fires South Korea to 2-1 comeback victory over CzechiaSouth Korea produced a stirring comeback to defeat Czechia 2-1 in their FIFA World Cup Group A opener in Guadalajara on Thursday, with Hwang In-beom starring in a memorable second-half turnaround.

England braces for history as Women's T20 World Cup promises a blockbusterThe 2026 Women's T20 World Cup promises to be a landmark event, with 12 teams competing in England and Wales. While Australia start as favourites, Harmanpreet Kaur's India, hosts England, defending champions New Zealand and South Africa are expected to be strong contenders.

Mexico's Quinones recreates Tshabalala's 2010 celebration in front of South AfricaJulian Quinones turned the clock back 16 years on the opening day of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, recreating Siphiwe Tshabalala's iconic celebration after scoring against South Africa as football's biggest tournament came full circle.

South Africa coach unhappy with red card call after crushing loss vs MexicoFIFA World Cup 2026: South Africa coach Hugo Broos accepted one red card but disputed another after Bafana Bafana's 2-0 defeat to Mexico, insisting his side played better than the scoreline suggested despite finishing the World Cup opener with nine men.

World Cup: Mexico boss Javier Aguirre demands more after victory over South AfricaMexico coach Javier Aguirre said his side had shaken off the nerves that came with opening the FIFA World Cup on home soil after a 2-0 win over nine-man South Africa in Group A at the Estadio Azteca on Thursday, but stressed that Mexico would need to raise their level in the matches ahead.

Wataru Endo retires from Japan duty after injury ends World Cup 2026 dreamFIFA World Cup 2026: Wataru Endo has withdrawn from Japan's World Cup squad and retired from international football after injury. His exit leaves Japan without their captain days before the tournament and opens a new leadership question.

Who is Wilton Sampaio? Referee goes viral after 3 red cards on World Cup Day 1FIFA World Cup 2026: Three red cards, a futuristic headset and countless memes. Brazilian referee Wilton Sampaio became one of the biggest stars of World Cup opening night as Mexico's fiery win over South Africa descended into chaos.

Who is Gilberto Mora? 17-year-old Mexico star breaks 96-year World Cup recordGilberto Mora's FIFA World Cup debut lasted only a few minutes, but it was enough to rewrite Mexican football history. The 17-year-old midfielder became Mexico's youngest-ever World Cup player as El Tri launched their home tournament with a convincing win over South Africa.

Sleepless and streamless: Indian fans face broadcast glitches on World Cup Day 1FIFA World Cup 2026: Indian football fans stayed up through the night for the return of the FIFA World Cup, only to find themselves battling buffering screens, app crashes and streaming complaints.

3 reds, 2 goals: Mexico sweep South Africa 2-0 in frenetic FIFA World Cup openerMexico opened the 2026 World Cup with a 2-0 win over South Africa in a match marred by three red cards. The result gave the co-hosts early control in Group A as Bafana Bafana were punished for their lacklustre performance.

World Cup referee headset sparks RoboCop memes during Mexico openerFIFA World Cup 2026: The football was barely minutes old when the referees stole the spotlight. The World Cup fans were left doing a double take after spotting Brazilian referee Wilton Sampaio wearing a futuristic headset that quickly sparked Xbox and RoboCop comparisons online.

Real Madrid announce Jose Mourinho as new manager to headline comeback eraReal Madrid have turned to a familiar face in their search for a revival, bringing Jose Mourinho back to the Santiago Bernabeu on a three-year deal. More than a decade after his first spell, "The Special One" is now tasked with leading another rebuild.

Mustafizur, Taskin star as Bangladesh secure maiden ODI series win vs AustraliaBangladesh beat Australia by five wickets in Dhaka to seal their first ODI series win over them. The result was sealed by a sensational early burst from Mustafizur Rahman and Taskin Ahmed with the new ball that wrecked the Aussie top order.

Shakira, Burna Boy get World Cup 2026 underway the Dai Dai wayShakira and Burna Boy opened the 2026 Men's FIFA World Cup with a live performance of "Dai Dai" at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City. The ceremony blended a tribute to Mexico's roots, a packed musical line-up and the stadium's historic third World Cup hosting role.

Highlights: South Korea and Mexico win their openers in Group AFIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights: After Mexico sealed a comprehensive win against South Africa, seven hours later, South Korea have also started their 2026 World Cup campaign with a huge victory against Czechia, coming from behind to secure three points and go second in Group A.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
